The below table outlines the figures for 2024-2025.Animal type 20242025Total Animals EuthanisedBovine186115301Equidae101Ovine96111207Porcine04747Poultry0149957149957Total283150230150513The significant increase in poultry euthanasia in 2025 reflects the impact of avian influenza outbreaks during that year, in contrast to 2024 when no outbreaks occurred.

To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to detail a timeline of his Department's review of the Public Angling Estate.
answer_text: The Department has completed an analysis of the current Public Angling Estate (PAE) including the identification of key issues and a qualitative assessment of PAE waters.The Department has engaged with stakeholders using a co-design approach and has developed the DAERA Public Angling Estate (PAE) Strategic Plan 2026–2031, setting out ten strategic objectives. The Plan was shared with stakeholders and published on the DAERA website in November 2025.The Department, in conjunction with stakeholders, will work through the Plan’s ten objectives over the next five years. Officials are currently working on objective 4 and 5 of the Plan to develop options for future PAE angling provision and delivery models for the management of fishing right at PAE waters. I will consider these options in Autumn 2026.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

To ask the Minister of Health, pursuant to AQW 35041/22-27, for an update on any discussions taking place with the Belfast Health and Social Care Trust in relation to car parking at the Royal Victoria Hospital.
answer_text: The primary focus of the engagement with the Belfast Health & Social Care Trust (BHSCT) on car parking in recent months has been in respect of the preparations for the implementation of the Hospital Parking Charges (Northern Ireland) Act 2022 that was due to come into operation on 12 May 2026.  More recently, and in response to the severe financial challenges facing the Health & Social Care sector, the emphasis of the discussions with the BHSCT has been on the preparations for the deferral of the 2022 Act through the Hospital Parking Charges Bill 2026, which was approved by the Assembly on 20 April 2026. The potential for additional funding for investment in enhanced car parking infrastructure at the Royal Victoria Hospital will be considered as part of the 10-year Capital Investment Plan for the Department of Health which will set out the capital projects and programmes that are expected to be funded over the next decade.  It will only be possible to take forward the development of this plan when the NI Executive has agreed the capital funding for Northern Ireland departments as part of the ongoing Budget process.

To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any engagement her Department has undertaken, along with Fermanagh and Omagh District Council, to promote the blueway around Enniskillen to encourage tourism and business opportunities.
answer_text: Tourism NI has been engaging over the past year with Fermanagh & Omagh District Council to progress the Enniskillen Blueway. Tourism NI is a founding member of the Blueway Partnership, an all-island collaboration involving Sport NI, Waterways Ireland, Fáilte Ireland and Sport Ireland. The partnership provides strategic oversight, guidance and accreditation for the development of Blueways across Ireland with the aim of creating high-quality, sustainable, water-based recreational trails that support outdoor activity, visitor experience, and regional economic development. The Enniskillen Blueway, led by Fermanagh & Omagh District Council, is well progressed and will seek to elevate the 2.8km looped trail around Ireland’s only island town. It is hoped that the Enniskillen Blueway will be the first one in the north, a real accolade for the town which is well placed to realise the potential of outdoor and activity tourism.Water-based activities and tours on Lough Erne feature regularly in promotional activity from Tourism NI. The recent dedicated Fermanagh Ireland’s Hidden Heartlands campaign was delivered through TV, Radio, Outdoor and social media activity. In total, the campaign delivered over 29 million advertising impacts and reached 62% of the population across Ireland. Tourism NI will continue to work with officials in Fermanagh & Omagh District Council to progress this unique tourism opportunity for the area.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail how often her Department carries out road assessments to monitor (i) travel patterns; (ii) modal shifts; and (iii) the wider economic impacts.
answer_text: Monitoring of road use, travel patterns and modal shift is central to the development of the new Transport Strategy and supporting Transport Plans. This is informed by regionwide surveys targeted to specific projects and plans, historic departmental survey data, transport models, forecasting tools and emerging smart sensor technologies, with the Department continuing to strengthen and improve its approach to data collection.Officials present analysis to me at key stages in the development of the Transport Plans and associated studies, ensuring I am appropriately informed to make evidencebased decisions on operational and policy matters.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 42632/22-27, to detail why Hydebank test centre has the highest level of vacancies of vehicle examiners, whilst Mallusk, which is also undergoing a capital project, has none.
answer_text: The Hydebank test centre was constructed as an additional facility to add more vehicle testing capacity to the network, which will need to be resourced when it becomes operational.The new Mallusk test centre is a replacement for the existing site and current staff will transfer to the new facility when it is ready to open. As a result, no additional vehicle examiners are required at this time, and no vacancies are currently recorded.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education whether his Department has considered providing funding to the Roots of Empathy programme in schools, to allow it to be delivered in all primary school settings.
answer_text: The Roots of Empathy programme is funded by the Public Health Agency (PHA) and is delivered across some primary and special schools. Research on the effectiveness of the programme generally points to its positive impact on pupils’ emotional development, empathy and relationship skills, however, there is no specific research suggesting its impacts on outcomes regarding violence against women and girls.As officials take forward development of a Healthy Relationships Action Plan (EVAWG Delivery Plan 2026-2028), the role that educationbased, evidenceinformed programmes can play in shaping positive attitudes and behaviours from an early age will be considered. A key aspect of the action plan will be to bring together all supporting resources and programmes that are available into the one space in order that schools and education settings can select the best fit for their children, young people and wider school community.  Schools have flexibility around the type of support provided for pupils and authority to use their individual budgets to fund external programmes based on their own assessment of the needs of their pupils.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to detail the number of times the sub-group established by the Procurement Board following the Northern Ireland Audit Office Major Capital Projects report, comprising the Permanent Secretaries of those Departments with high capital spending and chaired by his Department, has met within the 2022-27 Assembly mandate.
answer_text: There was no sub-group of Permanent Secretaries established by the Procurement Board following the NI Audit Office Report on Major Capital Projects in December 2019. The Procurement Board was stood down following its last meeting on 28 September 2022. In May 2024, the NICS Board agreed the Terms of Reference for the establishment of a sub-committee, tasked with overseeing the drafting and Executive approval of the Investment Strategy and the delivery of the Enabling Action Plan which includes 12 actions covering the themes of People, Process and Policy which are focussed on addressing issues that impact on the delivery of major capital projects. I have been informed that this Committee has met every 2 months since its establishment.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs, following his Ministerial Statement on 3 March 2026, for an update on the progress of the removal of the Statement of Regulatory Principles and Intent arrangements.
answer_text: In my oral statement on Wastewater Regulation and Enforcement delivered on the 3 March 2026, I set out my key interventions to strengthen the regulation and enforcement of wastewater activities and to reduce pollution across our water environment. Among these interventions was my proposal for the Northern Ireland Environment Agency to withdraw from the administrative Statement of Regulatory Principles and Intent (SORPI) with Northern Ireland Water. Following that statement, I have engaged further with the Minister for Infrastructure to discuss my proposal to withdraw from SORPI. I am seeking support from my Executive colleagues for this proposal with the aim of ensuring a consistent and level regulatory playing field for all organisations and individuals whose activities may impact the water environment.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

answer_text: The Driver & Vehicle Standards Agency (DVSA) in Britain is responsible for the vehicle recall scheme and they liaise with vehicle manufacturers on related issues and guidance.As my Department has no remit with regards to the vehicle recall and does not hold details of vehicles registered here affected by the recall scheme, there has been no engagement with the British Department for Transport on this matter.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether her Department has made any assessment on the number of vehicles in Northern Ireland still in need of being rectified, following the stop-drive action announced by Stellantis UK, for Citroen vehicles.
answer_text: My Department does not hold details of vehicles registered here affected by the recall scheme. The Driver & Vehicle Standards Agency (DVSA) in Britain are responsible for the vehicle recall scheme and they liaise with vehicle manufacturers on related issues and guidance.The DVSA Corporate Reputation Team has a dedicated email address for elected representatives: chief.executive@dvsa.gov.uk .

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs whether his Department has undertaken a review of climate change legislation in relation to the ability to meet 2030 targets.
answer_text: My focus remains firmly on the delivery of our legislative commitments which were approved by the Assembly.   As such my Department has not undertaken a review of the Climate Change Act (Northern Ireland) 2022 in relation to the ability to meet the 2030 target.Work on our first Climate Action Plan (CAP1) covering the period 2023-2027 is at an advanced development stage following an extensive public consultation. CAP1 sets out details of emissions reduction policies and proposals to meet our first Carbon Budget to ensure that we stay on track for meeting our emissions reduction targets. I intend to shortly bring the final CAP1 to the Executive for agreement. Separately work has also commenced on the development of our Second Climate Action Plan (CAP2) which covers the period 2028-2032 during which the 2030 target falls. I remain committed to working with my Executive colleagues to deliver credible Climate Action Plans which meet our agreed legislated commitments and in doing so ensure that we stay on the right pathway to deliver a just and fair transition to net zero.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether her Department has any plans to (i) reduce MOT centre opening hours on Sundays; and (ii) close MOT centres on Sundays.
answer_text: The Driver and Vehicle Agency (DVA) uses overtime to increase capacity to meet service demand and to provide cover for staff vacancies and absences. The availability of Sunday testing has contributed significantly to the DVA’s ability to meet its waiting time business plan targets.Over the last year, the DVA has reduced its reliance on Sunday opening to manage MOT waiting times. The opening of the new test centres at Hydebank and Mallusk will provide additional testing capacity. When these centres become fully operational, the DVA will review its policy on Sunday opening.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education whether his Department will (i) identify; and (ii) support, strategically important small rural schools.
answer_text: My Department does not currently have a definition of a ‘Strategically Important Small School’ however, the Sustainable Schools Policy does provide cover to determine if a small school needs to be retained and supported, or if other area solutions need to be explored.  Depending on local circumstances, the way forward in local communities may include retention, rationalisation, amalgamation, right-sizing of schools or innovative solutions to ensure continuity of provision.

To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 42152/22-27, to detail any action points that have been implemented, since the lessons learned exercise.
answer_text: One of the principal lessons identified by the Driver and Vehicle Agency from the previous contract was the importance of ensuring that the initial requirements set out in the tender documentation were sufficiently detailed, realistic, aligned with organisational needs, and enforceable should performance issues arise with the contractor.  In addition, a more rigorous approach was adopted for the evaluation process, supported by the Procurement Act 2023, which came into effect on 24 February 2025 and permits engagement with bidders as part of the process.These enhancements were subsequently incorporated into the procurement process for the new Vehicle Testing Solution contract, which was awarded on 11 March 2026. Work is now progressing to bring the Hydebank and Mallusk test centres into operation for vehicle testing, which is expected within the next year.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether her Department has carried out any assessment of the Climate Change Act (Northern Ireland) 2022 and her Department's ability to meet the targets by 2030.
answer_text: The Climate Change Act (NI) 2022 sets an interim target of at least a 48% reduction in net emissions by 2030. This target applies to departments collectively, meaning it is a shared obligation rather than an individual target on each department.Under the Act, specific departments are required to produce and publish sectoral plans setting out how specific sectors will contribute to meeting the emissions reduction target for 2030. My Department has responsibility for two such sectors – transport and infrastructure – and work to develop both sectoral plans is currently ongoing.In addition, DAERA is required to produce 5-year climate action plans to set out the policies and proposals that departments will implement to meet the corresponding carbon budget as well as set out how the emissions reduction targets will be achieved. The first budgetary period is 2023-2027, and as the transport sectoral lead, my Department has set out its approach for reducing transport related emissions within the draft Climate Action Plan (2023–27), which closed for consultation in October 2025. You may wish to note that although infrastructure is identified as a sector under the Climate Change Act (NI) 2022, its emissions cannot be measured separately, as they are embedded across sectors such as transport, energy and buildings. As a result, the draft Climate Action Plan does not include stand-alone infrastructure policies; instead, the associated emissions are addressed through the policies and proposals of other relevant sectors.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether her Department leases land to the National Car Parks company.

To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether her Department carries out any planning for worst case scenario situations, in terms of high fuel prices and the impact that has on vehicle owners and public transport.
answer_text: Fuel costs are not within my Department’s control.. Translink regularly reviews its fuel needs, operating assumptions and hedging arrangements to reduce exposure to market volatility. Translink currently has 80% of its diesel requirement for 2026/27 hedged, providing certainty on costs and delivering an estimated £8 million in cost avoidance to the public purse based on current market prices.The Department also tracks travel patterns, modal shift and the wider economic impacts on households, particularly those dependent on private vehicles for essential journeys. These assessments support budget planning, operational decisions and the development of policies that promote a resilient and accessible transport system.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail any cost to her Department arising from free parking on St Patrick's Day 2026, broken down by car park.
answer_text: Legislation was amended on 12 March 2026 to designate St Patrick’s Day as a non-charge day for on-street parking within Controlled Parking Zones (CPZs). This brought St Patrick’s Day into line with other existing non-charge days, namely New Year’s Day (or day in lieu), Easter Monday, 12 and 13 July (or days in lieu), and 25 and 26 December (or days in lieu). This amendment applies only to CPZ on-street charging and does not extend to my Department’s three Park and Ride sites which operate under different legislation.Local councils remain responsible for the enforcement and management of their own off-street car parks.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to detail the number of animal welfare convictions that have been secured by his Department, in the last five years.
answer_text: Under the Welfare of Animals Act (Northern Ireland) 2011, my Department is responsible for enforcing the provisions that relate to farmed animals only.  DAERA Veterinary Service inspectors have a range of enforcement options available when responding to an animal welfare case, in line with the DAERA Enforcement Policy. Depending on the circumstances, inspectors may provide advice, issue a warning, serve a legally binding Improvement Notice, or make a referral for a reduction or withholding of any subsidy support or animal disease compensation payments claimed. In more serious cases, they may initiate a prosecution.DAERA produces an annual report which sets out information on conviction data. These reports are published on the DAERA website and can be accessed at: www.daera-ni.gov.uk/articles/animal-welfare-statistics.The reports for 2024 and 2025 have not yet been published; however, I can confirm that four convictions were secured under the Welfare of Animals Act (Northern Ireland) 2011 by the Department in 2024, and seven convictions were secured in 2025.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to detail when the criteria was changed to exclude churches from the Dormant Assets for All funding scheme.
answer_text: No changes to the criteria, in relation to religious institutions, were made for the Dormant Assets for All funding scheme. I refer the member to my answer to AQW 42547/22-27 for further information.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance the detail the reason churches cannot apply for the Dormant Assets for All funding scheme.
answer_text: The Dormant Assets NI Fund aims to provide flexible support to increase capacity, grow resilience and encourage sustainability within the Voluntary, Community and Social Enterprise (VCSE) sector here. In developing the Fund’s criteria, the scheme administrator, the National Lottery Community Fund, engaged with stakeholders and drew on the experience and lessons from other funds. Against that background, and in keeping with the Fund’s particular focus and objectives in relation to helping VCSE organisations build their skills, capacity and resilience, it does not provide direct funding to religious institutions such as churches, mosques and synagogues.  However, it is open to faith-based community organisations that are active in the VCSE Sector. This approach was discussed with the VCSE Faith Forum in 2021, and members understood and accepted the distinction. The Dormant Assets for All programme, Phase Three of the Dormant Assets NI Fund, opened on 18 June 2025 and will run for two years. It applies the same eligibility criteria in relation to religious institutions and, to date, a number of faith-based organisations have been successful in securing funding through this programme.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to detail any measures being introduced in his Planning Department to improve planning performance and service, and to address delays in planning consultation responses.
answer_text: The Northern Ireland Environment Agency (NIEA) has developed and is implementing a Planning Improvement Plan. This plan includes a range of measures to improve planning performance and customer service, and covers 5 strands: technical advice and guidance; caseload management; monitoring and reporting on performance; process and IT systems; and, communication and stakeholder engagement. NIEA continues to work in collaboration with the Department for Infrastructure cross-governmental Planning Statutory Consultee Forum which aims to drive forward continuous improvements in the planning system.The delivery of this improvement plan, in conjunction with filling scientific vacancies in the next 6 months, will assist in addressing the delays in planning consultation responses.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to detail the number of animal welfare incidents his Department's staff have attended, in the last five years, broken down by constituency.
answer_text: Under the Welfare of Animals Act (Northern Ireland) 2011, my Department is responsible for enforcing the provisions that relate to farmed animals only.  For the purposes of this response, “animal welfare incidents” has been interpreted as farmed animal welfare concerns reported to my Department by members of the public and other authorities for investigation. The information provided below relates solely to the number of initial attendances undertaken in response to farmed animal welfare incidents. Subsequent follow up visits linked to the same incident have not been included.Total figures are detailed, as figures are not recorded on a Constituency basis.	Year	The number of initial attendances  to farmed animal welfare reports undertaken by the Department	2020	148	2021	124	2022	139	2023	179	2024	174	The information for 2025 is not yet available.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs whether his Department will increase the number of fishing pegs on the Public Angling Estate in Lough Erne.
answer_text: My Department keeps the level of service provision on the Public Angling Estate at Lough Erne under review. Existing levels of provision are being maintained. Any future changes will be considered following the conclusion of the review of the Public Angling Estate.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether the reasons for missed appointments are captured, in each Health and Social Care Trust.
To ask the Minister of Health to detail the (i) number of; and (ii) cost of, missed appointments, in the last financial year, broken down by Health and Social Care Trust.
To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs whether any assessment has been made of access to the angling estate in Lough Erne.
answer_text: Access to the Public Angling Estate at Lough Erne is considered through routine inspections and maintenance of the existing angling access infrastructure provided at sites.
