question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether all Health and Social Care Trusts use the Royal Mail economy service.
answer_text: My Department has not carried out an assessment of the postal service currently utilised by Health and Social Care Trusts.However, to minimise any patient inconvenience, Trusts have implemented a dual notification system for appointment reminders. In addition to sending appointment letters to patients, Trusts also use text message reminders to notify patients of their upcoming appointments in most services. Furthermore, the Encompass system is now fully operational across Northern Ireland. All patients and service users will be able to securely access a subset of their Health and Social Care record via the My Care application, which provides secure online access to personal health information such as appointment letters, clinic letters, medication details, and some test results. They can also view appointments and track the progress of their treatment.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail the number of people in Fermanagh and South Tyrone who have received treatment for (i) polycystic ovary syndrome; and (ii) endometriosis, in each of the last five years.
answer_text: The Western Health and Social Care Trust (WHSCT) are unable to provide figures for polycystic ovary syndrome (PCOS) as patients with this condition attend general gynaecology clinics.  There is no dedicated PCOS clinic. The WHSCT can provide the number of women attending the complex endometriosis clinic.  However, this would not provide the total number of patients who have received treatment for endometriosis, as many women attend general gynaecology clinics for management and treatment of low to moderate endometriosis.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail the number of staff employed within the Western Health and Social Care Trust for endometriosis services, including where they are based.
To ask the Minister of Health whether sufficient funding is available to implement full endometriosis services in the Western Health and Social Care Trust.
answer_text: The development of endometriosis services remains one of my key priorities for improving women’s health. Officials expect the submission of the required business cases in the coming weeks. However, current funding is insufficient to deliver the full capacity required under the agreed regional pathway, and expansion will therefore need to proceed on a phased basis as additional funding becomes available.In 2025/26, £3.5m has been allocated for gynaecology service development. This includes the expansion of ETriage across all Trusts, the introduction of Direct to Test services with increased sonography capacity, enhanced procedural nurse capacity, and the expansion of Phase 1 Endometriosis services. The outcome of bids for additional gynaecology investment from the £80m Elective Care funding, expected in April 2026, is still awaited.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health for an update on (i) the new model of care for endometriosis services; and (ii) the establishment of regional tertiary centres in the Western Health and Social Care Trust.
To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the number of jobs created in Fermanagh and South Tyrone, broken down by sector, in 2024-25.
answer_text: Invest NI proactively publishes a full breakdown of financial support offered to every business over the past 5 years on the NI Open Data online portal which can be accessed from the following link: Invest NI Open Data.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance whether any Barnett consequential funding will be ring-fenced for specialist domestic abuse services in Northern Ireland, including refuge provision, community-based services, and children and young people's support.
answer_text: Barnett consequentials are unhypothecated, it is a matter for the Executive to agree how such funding is allocated including whether funding should be ring-fenced for specific purposes.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to detail any assurances that can be provided that any Barnett consequential funding received will directly support frontline domestic abuse services, rather than being absorbed into general departmental budgets.
answer_text: Barnett consequentials are unhypothecated, it is a matter for the Executive to agree how this funding will be allocated as part of the multi-year Budget. However, in my draft Budget proposals I have recommended that the Executive sets aside earmarked funding of £3.6 million in 2026-27, £5.8 million in 2027-28 and £5.8 million in 2028-28 for the Executive Office to take forward the Ending Violence Against Women and Girls programme.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance whether Northern Ireland will receive any Barnett consequential funding, following the announcement from the UK Government on 15 December 2025, for additional funding to support victims of domestic abuse and violence against women and girls, including an additional £19 million for safe accommodation support in England over the next three years.
answer_text: This announcement will be funded from the Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government’s Spending Review outcome and as such does not give rise to additional Barnett consequentials for the Executive.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the level of foreign direct investment across each constituency, in the last five years.
answer_text: Invest NI proactively publishes a full breakdown of support offered to businesses over the past 5 years on the NI Open Data online portal. The portal contains the following information which will facilitate access to the data requested:	Portal | Invest NI Total Tables 1 April 2020 and 31 March 2025	Provides a complete picture of Invest NI support through summary tables that aggregate both published and non-published data (indirect support, like advice and guidance, and offers that cannot be released due to data protection issues). This information is broken down into several different variables, including council and constituency areas.	Portal | Invest NI Financial Offers of Assistance (Detail)	Provides detail on Individual offers of direct financial assistance issued by Invest NI to businesses between 1 April 2020 and 31 March 2025. Does not include indirect support, like advice and guidance, or offers that cannot be released due to data protection issues.	Portal | Invest NI Open Data Guidance	Provides guidance on the data contained in 1 and 2 above.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail (i) the average time taken for a patient's medical information to be passed to their own Health and Social Care Trust following care in another Trust; and (ii) any effect on the time taken following the roll-out of encompass.
answer_text: Prior to the implementation of encompass, a variety of electronic health-care systems operated within individual Health and Social Care Trusts, alongside a small number of regional systems used across all Trusts. There were no mechanisms to allow information to be transferred electronically between these disparate systems. For the majority of systems, transfer of information relied on the movement of manual records.  The time required to transfer these records between Trusts was not measured or recorded. The rollout of encompass has replaced paper records and disparate systems with one regional electronic health record, giving authorised clinicians immediate access to a patient’s information regardless of which Trust delivered prior care. This directly reduces any delays which may have been experienced previously with fragmented paper based or siloed electronic systems.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether she has met or is planning to meet with the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs, following his Ministerial Statement to the Assembly on Tuesday 3 March 2026, which signalled his intention to remove Statement of Regulatory Principles and Intent arrangements.
answer_text: I am grateful to Minister Muir for his ongoing engagement on his proposal for the NIEA to withdraw from the current Statement of Regulatory Principles and Intent (SORPI) with NI Water, and connected regulatory issues detailed in his statement to the Assembly on Tuesday 3rd March 2026. I met Minister Muir on Monday 9th March to discuss these issues further, following previous meetings with Minister Muir on the topic on 2nd June and 17th November last year. In my view it is essential that a managed approach is taken to implementation of this proposal and other regulatory changes outlined by Minister Muir. This can ensure that operational impacts for NI Water and any cross-cutting impacts for the Executive, including in respect of housing, can be clearly understood.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice to detail any elements of the Strategy to End Violence Against Women and Girls that have been implemented by her Department.
answer_text: Ending violence against women and girls remains a key priority for me as the Minister of Justice. Addressing this complex and deeply rooted issue requires a whole of society and whole of government approach, which is why the Strategic Framework to End Violence Against Women and Girls (EVAWG) is appropriately led by The Executive Office, with all Departments contributing to delivery through the cross-departmental Delivery Plan.The Department of Justice is responsible for a number of actions within that Plan, primarily focused on prevention, public awareness and improving victims’ understanding of the justice process. However, in addition there is a wide programme of work and new measures that my Department has progressed which are also delivering shared outcomes against the EVAWG Framework as they are aimed at addressing sexual offending and domestic abuse, and supporting the victims in these cases, of whom it is acknowledged the majority are women and girls. My Department has been working with the Police Service of Northern Ireland and The Executive Office to deliver the Power to Change public awareness campaign. The first phase of the campaign launched on 28 January 2025 and seeks to challenge misogynistic attitudes and behaviours that can underpin sexual offending and wider gender-based violence, including violence against women and girls. The campaign included radio advertising, social media content and billboard and Adshell posters across Northern Ireland, alongside community engagement activity led by the PSNI. All communications signpost to the dedicated Power to Change website which provides information, guidance and advice.My Department also commenced initial work on the My Justice Journey project, which aimed to provide a digital portal to improve victims’ understanding of the justice process and the supports available to them. Work on this project was paused in order to progress other high priority work intended to protect those at risk from domestic abuse, including work to establish new Domestic Abuse Risk Response (DARR) arrangements to replace the existing Multi-Agency Risk Assessment Conferences (MARAC). In the interim, Victim Support NI has developed a comprehensive online resource outlining the victims’ journey through the justice system which delivers the same intended outcome.More broadly, the Department contributes to the objectives of the Strategy through its wider programme of work to tackle domestic abuse and support victims and survivors. This includes delivery of the Domestic and Sexual Abuse Strategy 2024–2031, which is jointly led by the Department of Justice and the Department of Health. The Domestic and Sexual Abuse Strategy links closely with the Ending Violence Against Women and Girls Strategic Framework; however, the EVAWG framework addresses a broader range of harms, including the societal attitudes and behaviours that underpin violence against women and girls. For that reason, both strategies play complementary b

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether the Driver and Vehicle Agency has undertaken a lessons learnt exercise, following the severance of the Worldwide Environmental Products Limited contracts for MOT centres.
answer_text: The Driver and Vehicle Agency undertook a lessonslearned exercise following the termination of the previous vehicle testing equipment contract. This informed and strengthened the procurement approach for the new Vehicle Testing Solution contract, which was awarded on 11 March 2026.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice to detail (i) the Red, Amber, Green status of the elements relating to her Department in the Strategy to End Violence Against Women and Girls; and (ii) a timeline of delivery for those elements.
answer_text: The Ending Violence Against Women and Girls (EVAWG) Strategic Framework and Delivery plan is led by The Executive Office. The Department of Justice contributes to the delivery of EVAWG outcomes through a number of actions within the associated EVAWG Action Plan (which are focused primarily on prevention, public awareness and improving victims’ experience of the justice system) as well as through delivery of my Department’s wider responsibilities, including the Domestic and Sexual Abuse Strategy. In relation to the elements for which the Department of Justice has responsibility, the current position is as follows.My Justice Journey – RAG Status: RedWork on the My Justice Journey project was paused in order to progress other high priority work intended to protect those at risk from domestic abuse, including work to establish new Domestic Abuse Risk Response (DARR) arrangements to replace the existing Multi-Agency Risk Assessment Conferences (MARAC). However, in the meantime, I am pleased to report that Victim Support NI (VSNI) has developed an excellent criminal justice system victim journey resource on their website - The Victims' Journey - Victim Support NI. Timeline: Work was initially paused temporarily while resources were focused on another high priority project. The Department will consider next steps, including whether there is an ongoing priority need for My Justice Journey in light of the VSNI resource.Power to Change Campaign – RAG Status: GreenMy Department is working with the PSNI and the Executive Office to deliver the Power to Change public awareness campaign. The first phase of the campaign launched on 28 January 2025 and it aims to tackle the underpinning misogynistic behaviours and attitudes that can fuel sexual offending and gender-based violence, including violence against women and girls. The campaign consisted of a series of radio advertisements, content on social media and billboard and Adshell posters. The PSNI is also leading community engagement that will utilise the campaign assets to start face to face conversations with local community groups and youth forums. All communications signpost to the bespoke Power To Change website that includes a range of information, guidance and advice. To date, over 4,000 people have visited the website and the overall reach of the creative content on social media was 3,100,000 with 1.2M impressions on TikTok; 2.4M impressions on YouTube with videos viewed over 70,000 times. The second phase will launch in May 2026 and will focus on challenging the misogynistic messages conveyed through some social media channels.Timeline: Phase 1 launched in January 2025, with Phase 2 due to launch in May 2026.More broadly, my Department continues to take forward a range of initiatives aimed at improving the experience of victims and strengthening the justice system’s response to all victims of domestic and sexual abuse and other forms of violence against women and girls. This includes ongoing work under

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether any preparatory work is underway for the tender process of roads maintenance contracts.
answer_text: Road maintenance is predominantly delivered through eighty measuredterm contracts currently in place, which will be replaced over the next two years as part of my Department’s Procurement Enhancement Programme.Preparation for future procurement activity is being undertaken through the planning and implementation of the programme of Frameworks and Contracts, working to key objectives such as improving efficiency, supporting competition, enhancing resilience, reducing the risk of procurement challenge, and maintaining a strong and sustainable local construction industry, including Small and Medium-sized Enterprises.On completion of the procurement of each contract phase, my officials undertake a review to identify any lessons learned. The findings from these exercises inform future procurement exercises, supporting continuous improvement in our approach to roads maintenance procurement.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail (i) the number of job vacancies that exist in each DFI Roads Division, as of February 2026; and (ii) any steps she is taking to address any shortfall in staff within the Divisions.
answer_text: My Department continues to face significant resource challenges in the recruitment and retention of staff. We face particular challenges attracting those with professional qualifications, such as Civil Engineers, which has impacted our ability to deliver essential services and infrastructure projects. The number of Full Time Equivalent (FTE) vacancies across the Transport and Road Asset Management (TRAM) Group within DfI is shown in the table below. This is based on the optimal number of staff required should suitable funding be available. DivisionNumber of Vacancies (FTE)Northern 38.5Southern43.3Eastern40.2Western33.7Total155.7To address the vacancies, my Department has established a People Sub-Committee to ensure that workforce capacity risks are identified and managed.To help to build long-term resilience, we are using an innovative recruitment such as using the already successfully established Skills Academy and piloting ‘Talentdog’ which will speed up the recruitment of new industrial staff, enhancing resource capability to target directly towards pothole repair.The Trainee Civil Engineer Assistant Skills Academy and Industrial Worker recruitment campaigns have just been launched, and the PTO Apprentice competition will follow later in Spring 2026. These recruitments are expected to deliver about 130 people into critical front line positions, many of which are in the Divisions.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on the consultation regarding biennial MOT testing.
answer_text: A synopsis of responses from the consultation has been completed and this will be published in due course.My officials are currently finalising a detailed paper for my consideration on the future of MOT testing here.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether her Department undertook a lessons learnt exercise, following the tender process of the current roads maintenance contracts, for each of the DFI Roads Divisions.
answer_text: Road maintenance is predominantly delivered through eighty measuredterm contracts currently in place, which will be replaced over the next two years as part of my Department’s Procurement Enhancement Programme.Preparation for future procurement activity is being undertaken through the planning and implementation of the programme of Frameworks and Contracts, working to key objectives such as improving efficiency, supporting competition, enhancing resilience, reducing the risk of procurement challenge, and maintaining a strong and sustainable local construction industry, including Small and Medium-sized Enterprises.On completion of the procurement of each contract phase, my officials undertake a review to identify any lessons learned. The findings from these exercises inform future procurement exercises, supporting continuous improvement in our approach to roads maintenance procurement.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether any work is currently underway in her Department to extend the Temporary Exemption Certificates, in place for MOT.
answer_text: I recently announced an extension of Temporary Exemption Certificates (TECs) for a further year to maintain MOT waiting times until the new test centres at Hydebank and Mallusk open for vehicle testing, which is expected within the next year.From 1 June 2026, private cars first registered between 1 June 2021 and 31 May 2022 or between 1 June 2019 and 31 May 2020, which have a valid MOT issued by the Driver and Vehicle Agency, will have a one-year TEC automatically applied from the date their current MOT certificate expires. This will allow motorists to continue to drive their vehicles legally, provided the vehicles are kept in a roadworthy condition, are taxed and insured.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail the number of paediatric dietitians, broken down by Health and Social Care Trust.
To ask the Minister of Health whether he has had any discussions with Executive colleagues in relation to the Minimum Standards for Childminding and Day Care for Children Under Age 12.
To ask the Minister of Health for an update on the public consultation on Minimum Standards for Childminding and Day Care for Children Under Age 12.
answer_text: My Department will be publishing the consultation response document in the coming weeks.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail the waiting times for paediatric dietitians, broken down by Health and Social Care Trust.
To ask the Minister of Health to detail (i) the level of GP Out of Hours provision in Fermanagh and South Tyrone; and (ii) how this compares to targets for service provision.
answer_text: Western Urgent Care (WUC) is commissioned to provide the GP Out of Hours (OOHs) service across the Western area.  The service is intended for patients who require urgent medical attention but cannot safely wait until their GP practice reopens. The local population is encouraged to utilise the service appropriately for urgent needs. The GP OOHs service is available Monday-Friday from 6pm to 8am the next morning, and 24 hours a day at weekends and during public holidays.  All patients can contact WUC via a single telephone number, there are no telephone numbers available to contact bases directly.  Patients who contact the service will be triaged by clinical staff via a telephone or video consultation. Staff may be based at any one of the service’s locations in Derry/Londonderry, Enniskillen, Limavady, Strabane or Omagh.  Many patients can be safely and effectively clinically triaged by either a Nurse Adviser, Prescribing Pharmacist, an Advanced Nurse Practitioner (ANPs) or a GP.  From April to December 2025, 66% of WUC patient contacts were managed with advice by telephone or video consultation, 30% required a base visit and 4% required a home visit.On occasions when there is reduced cover in the Enniskillen base, patient needs continue to be met via the cross-base triage model.  Prescribers in other bases can arrange for any patient prescriptions to be delivered to the South West Acute Hospital (SWAH) base when required.There are no targets for the level of cover in individual areas.  Western Urgent Care provides cover for the full commissioned out of hours operating period.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs whether any fines related to the removal of Statement of Regulatory Principles and Intent arrangements would be payable to (i) his Department; and (ii) another department.
answer_text: The Courts currently impose fines for offences under The Water (NI) Order 1999 up to a maximum of £20,000 on conviction in a Magistrates Court and unlimited, on indictment, in a Crown Court.  Any fines related to the removal of the Statement of Regulatory Principles and Intent would be paid to the Department of Justice. On prosecution, the Court may award the department its costs and/ or remediation costs. Remediation costs are set aside for improvement or other agreed works in respect of the river impacted by any incident.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs whether he will jointly fund the event duration monitors at NI Water overspill sites, given that this would provide better real time data on potential pollution incidents.
answer_text: The funding of event duration monitors is a matter for NI Water and the Department of Infrastructure. I do agree that the installation of event duration monitors would provide better information in relation to sewage spills and that is why I have recently called on NI Water and the Department of Infrastructure to speed up their programme of installation.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to detail the number of times he has met NI Water to discuss his Statement of Regulatory Principles and Intent arrangements, since coming into office.
answer_text: Northern Ireland Water is a Non-Departmental Public Body (NDPB) sponsored by the Department for Infrastructure, which is also the Government Owned Company’s sole shareholder. Accordingly, I have met and discussed my department’s proposal to withdraw from the Statement of Regulatory Principles and Intent (SORPI) with the Infrastructure Minister on three occasions. NI Water was not present at these meetings. My officials have engaged with NI Water on this matter as part of the Department’s ongoing governance arrangements. Additionally, my department consulted both DfI and NI Water on SORPI between April and July 2025. Both the Minister for Infrastructure and NI Water provided responses to this consultation, which have been carefully considered, and discussed during ongoing engagement with the Infrastructure Minister.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the amount of extra capacity Ballygawley Wastewater Treatment plant now has, following £8 million investment to upgrade the site.
answer_text: The recently completed upgrade project has increased the treatment capacity of the Ballygawley wastewater treatment works by 33%. The original facility was designed to serve a population equivalent of around 1,500. The upgrades increased treatment capacity of 1,993 population equivalent. This will enable the removal of restrictions on new development which were in place in Ballygawley due to wastewater capacity constraints.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to detail when he brought his Statement of Regulatory Principles and Intent proposals to the Executive.
answer_text: The member will appreciate that all aspects of Executive business are regarded as confidential and, accordingly, it would not be appropriate for me to comment further on the conduct of any Executive business.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to detail any discussions he has had with (i) HRMC; or (ii) HM Treasury, regarding the proposals to withdraw the wear and tear tax allowance for childminders.
answer_text: I refer the member to the answer to AQW 41828/22-27.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any assessment her Department has undertaken to ascertain the impact that the removal of the wear and tear tax allowance will have upon child carers in Northern Ireland.
answer_text: The removal of this tax allowance will initially affect childminders with qualifying income of over £50,000, who will be mandated into Making Tax Digital (MTD) for Income Tax from April 2026. This new policy will then be further phased in to include childminders with an income of between £20,000 and £30,000 from April 2028.  		I recognise the increased administrative burden that this will present to many small businesses and that childminders will now have to claim tax relief by deducting their actual business expenses for wear and tear items. 	My Department published research in September 2025 that looked at key issues impacting the financial sustainability of childcare providers and specific targeted business support requirements (https://www.economy-ni.gov.uk/publications/targeted-business-support-childcare-providers-market-research).  My Department is leading on the design and delivery of Recommendation 1: ‘the creation of a specialised and on-going childcare business advisor role or service’.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to detail any discussions he is having with (i) the UK Government; and (ii) other relevant stakeholders, regarding potential fertiliser price increases due to the ongoing situation in the Middle East.
answer_text: I am concerned about potential fertiliser price increases due to the ongoing situation in the Middle East.  I fully appreciate the concern within the farming community with the prospect of increased costs and volatility.  It is not possible at this stage to determine the longer term impacts but I will continue to monitor the market situation.  My officials have also been discussing the potential impacts on the agricultural sector with UK Government officials.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities whether he is having any discussions with (i) his Executive colleagues; and (ii) his UK counterparts, regarding support for home owners who may be affected by oil and gas price increases as a result of the current situation in the Middle East.
answer_text: Lead responsibility for oil and gas prices falls to the Department for the Economy.I recognise the pressures rising energy costs place on households, particularly given Northern Ireland’s reliance on home heating oil.Discretionary Support may assist those facing sudden and unaffordable heating costs, including through interestfree loans or, in specific circumstances, nonrepayable grants. The NI Energy Advice Service, funded by my Department and delivered by the Housing Executive, continues to provide free impartial guidance.  This service has helped many households access support such as the Affordable Warmth Scheme, which has invested over £140 million since 2014 to improve more than 31,000 lowincome homes.Longerterm action is set out in the Warm Healthy Homes Strategy, with a new fund launching next year to support lowincome and vulnerable households living in the least energyefficient homes, informed by public consultation planned for Spring 2026.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to detail any assessment his Department has undertaken to ascertain the impact that the removal of the wear and tear tax allowance will have on on child minders in Northern Ireland.
answer_text: I refer the member to the answer to AQW 41828/22-27.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether she will consider any support for businesses that are dependent on road fuels, should oil and gas prices increase, in light of the emerging situation in the Middle East.
answer_text: I am very concerned about the impact of recent increases in oil prices on households and businesses here, arising from the conflict in the Middle East. Along with Executive colleagues I continue to make the case to the British Government for more support for both households and businesses.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether she intends to meet with the Utility Regulator, following the situation in the Middle East, regarding oil and gas prices.
answer_text: I have met with the Utility Regulator regarding energy prices. My Department continues to monitor the situation in the middle east, and to work closely with the Utility Regulator to ensure that energy prices reflect real market conditions and the actual cost of supplying energy to consumers.The Utility Regulator is responsible for regulating the electricity and gas markets in the North, providing consumer protections and oversight. Home heating oil is sold in a competitive but unregulated retail market and does not fall within their statutory remit.  	Trading Standards plays a key role in protecting consumers from illegal trading practices, including unfair pricing, while supporting legitimate businesses. It also provides guidance to oil retailers on their obligations and advice to consumers on raising complaints.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any work her Department has underway to evaluate the situation in the Middle East in order to plan for any future gas and oil price increases.
answer_text: I have spoken directly with the British Energy Minister, Michael Shanks who has provided reassurance that there is no short-term risk to supply. He acknowledged, however, that the conflict is impacting the price of home heating oil and that over 60% of households here use oil to heat their homes.  I have asked Minister Shanks for support for consumers similar to the support provided in 2022 following the Ukraine invasion and have been assured that the British government continues to monitor the situation closely and is looking at what further support may be needed.The Consumer Council actively monitors heating oil prices here and publishes weekly average prices. I encourage consumers to check the CCNI website and shop around between suppliers. Also, the Trading Standards Service works to protect consumers from illegal trading practices, such as unfair pricing, while supporting legitimate businesses.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether she will consider any further support for homeowners, should oil and gas prices significantly increase, in light of the emerging situation in the Middle East.

To ask the Minister for the Economy whether she has had, or plans to have, any discussions with her UK counterparts in relation to energy price increases arising from the situation in the Middle East, to establish the impact on households.
answer_text: When the conflict in the Middle East, began, the reduction in electricity bills of around £30 per year had already been announced and delivery was being progressed by my Department.Many of our households and businesses are already under pressure from the cost-of-living crisis, and this new energy shock is compounding those pressures. I am particularly concerned about the impact on low-income and vulnerable households.Consumer Council figures show that the cost of oil for households has nearly doubled since 26th February – for 500 litre purchases the cost is almost £300 extra.I have pressed the British government for support and I continue to do so. I spoke to the Department for Energy Security and Net Zero’s Energy Minister, Michael Shanks on 4th March to highlight the impact of the immediate rise in heating oil costs here.I subsequently spoke and wrote to Department for Energy Security and Net Zero’s Energy Consumers Minister, Martin McCluskey about the urgent need for support.The £17 million announced by the British government is clearly inadequate, therefore, it is important that whatever support is available, or becomes available going forward, is targeted at those in most need.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether her Department has reported any road signage with graffiti damage to the PSNI, over the last five years.
answer_text: My Department has no record of graffiti being reported to the PSNI over the last five years.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail a breakdown of the costs relating to damaged road signs, during the last three financial years.
answer_text: The cost of replacing damaged road signs is contained within my Department’s funding allocation for traffic maintenance; however, expenditure on replacing damaged signs is not separately categorised or recorded.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether her Department records graffiti on damaged road signs and categorises it depending upon the nature of the graffiti.
answer_text: My Department carries out regular inspections of all public roads and footways to ensure that essential response maintenance is identified and completed as necessary. During these inspections, defaced signage is categorised as Traffic Sign Graffiti (TSG) on my Department’s Maintenance system. This category does not provide the facility to indicate a description of the type of graffiti, and any mechanism to do so would involve significant changes to the current computerised system which would have an impact on the already limited resources available for the essential maintenance of our road network.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the number of houses on a private street that have been adopted, in the last five years, broken down by local council area.
answer_text: Information on the Highway Network, including a map displaying all adopted roads, is available on the following Open Data NI page:https://www.opendatani.gov.uk/@transportni/highway-network.The data can be downloaded and filtered using the ADOPTION_STATUS_NAME column to show current adopted road sections and provides the digital length. This is a 'live' link and is updated each evening to include any new road sections. My Department does not hold records of the number of houses on roads.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail any projected increase of costs for the A4 Enniskillen Southern Bypass, due to the delay in works commencing.
answer_text: Once the procurement process has concluded and a preferred bidder is confirmed, the Department will be in a position to assess the final tendered costs, including any inflationary impacts , and to confirm the timescale for progression of the scheme.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the number of housing developments known to her Department, that are situated on private streets and therefore are unable to be adopted.
answer_text: Information on the Highway Network, including a map displaying all adopted roads, is available on the following Open Data NI page:https://www.opendatani.gov.uk/@transportni/highway-network.The data can be downloaded and filtered using the ADOPTION_STATUS_NAME column to show current adopted road sections and provides the digital length. This is a 'live' link and is updated each evening to include any new road sections. My Department does not hold records of the number of houses on roads.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the number of houses that are situated on private streets, broken down by local council area.
answer_text: Information on the Highway Network, including a map displaying all adopted roads, is available on the following Open Data NI page:https://www.opendatani.gov.uk/@transportni/highway-network.The data can be downloaded and filtered using the ADOPTION_STATUS_NAME column to show current adopted road sections and provides the digital length. This is a 'live' link and is updated each evening to include any new road sections. My Department does not hold records of the number of houses on roads.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether the A4 Enniskillen Southern Bypass is exempt from inflationary costs.
answer_text: Once the procurement process has concluded and a preferred bidder is confirmed, the Department will be in a position to assess the final tendered costs, including any inflationary impacts , and to confirm the timescale for progression of the scheme.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether he has (i) received; and (ii) considered, a business case for the commissioning of a new emergency general surgery service at the South West Acute Hospital.
To ask the Minister of Health to detail any meetings and engagements he has had with the Western Health and Social Care Trust, since he took up office, including the purpose of each meeting.
To ask the Minister of Health to detail any next steps he plans to take whenever he receives the Western Health and Social Care Trust vision plan.
To ask the Minister of Health whether he has been in communication with the Western Health and Social Care Trust for an update on the vision plan he requested in July 2025.
To ask the Minister of Health whether he provided a timescale for the Western Health and Social Care Trust to complete their vision plan, which he requested in July 2025.
To ask the Minister of Health (i) for his assessment of his Department's regional balance policy; and (ii) to detail any actions he has delivered to support regional balance, in the last 12 months.
answer_text: The Department of Health seeks to promote equitable access to health and social care services across Northern Ireland through a combination of resource allocation mechanisms and service reform.A central resource allocation tool used by the Strategic Planning and Performance Group (SPPG) is the NI Capitation Formula. This statistical model determines fair shares of funding provided to HSC Trusts and is designed to distribute resources in line with the relative need of local populations. The formula is constructed around Programmes of Care, each incorporating:Relevant population – the specific service user group.Age/gender weighting – recognising demographic impacts on service demand.Additional needs weighting – accounting for factors such as deprivation that increase service need.Adjustments for rurality and economies of scale are applied to reflect variations in delivery costs across areas. SPPG undertakes equity reviews comparing planned and actual expenditure against these fair shares. These reviews inform decisions on funding strategy and highlight any gaps between assessed need and Trust baseline allocations.While locality models align with Trust boundaries, the formula is not intended to represent Trustlevel entitlements, as service users frequently cross boundaries to access care.Sustained financial pressures in recent years have limited the ability to shift baseline allocations. However, where possible, new investment such as demographic funding has been directed to support movement towards capitationbased equity. In addition, savings targets have been skewed in favour of those Trusts where analysis shows they are funded below fairshare levels.A key aspect of our reform agenda and our recently published HSC Reset Plan is to improve access to healthcare and reduce regional variation in service provision. Current pressures across the Health and Social Care system highlight the need for change, with the overall aim of ensuring that people receive appropriate services in a timely way and that outcomes improve.A threeyear improvement plan, published in December 2024, sets out actions across primary care, hospital services, social care, productivity, and patient safety. The plan includes ambitious objectives for areas such as mental health and elective care, and places increased emphasis on reducing health inequalities.The HSC Reset Plan published in July 2025 lays out how we will deliver, in specific terms. It includes commitments to deliver health and social care in a joined-up way, with a whole system approach. This will include considering regional solutions and reducing unwarranted variations in practice, supporting the principle of fair and equitable access to services across the region.  A further key priority of Reset is shifting more care into community settings, reducing reliance on acute hospitals. Work is advancing on the development of the Neighbourhood Model of Care, which aims to support people and deliver care within local commun

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education (i) for his assessment of his Department's regional balance policy; and (ii) to detail any actions he has delivered to support regional balance, in the last 12 months.
answer_text: Sustaining a highquality and inclusive education system is a core priority for my Department and this underpins my approach to regional balance. My Department is committed to supporting every child and young person across Northern Ireland. The Department’s policies, strategies and plans are subject to equality screening and rural needs impact assessments. Therefore, although my Department does not have a specific regional balance policy, all decisions are taken in the best interests of all children and young person.Within the constraints of its Resource Budget allocation, my Department makes every effort to protect frontline service delivery. This includes prioritising resources for schools, special educational needs provision, and other core services that directly contribute to equitable educational outcomes across Northern Ireland.My Department and its Arm’s Length Bodies are also engaged at a local level through community planning partnerships. I will continue to work with my Executive colleagues to ensure that all children and young people across Northern Ireland are treated fairly in order that they may reach their full potential.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister (i) for their assessment of their Department's regional balance policy; and (ii) to detail any actions they have delivered to support regional balance, in the last 12 months.

To ask the Minister for Communities (i) for his assessment of his Department's regional balance policy; and (ii) to detail any actions he has delivered to support regional balance, in the last 12 months.
answer_text: My Department seeks to achieve regional balance by ensuring that equality, good relations and rural needs are integral to policy development and programme delivery. All strategies and plans are informed by robust evidence of need and comply with statutory duties, including section 75 of the Northern Ireland Act 1998 and the Rural Needs Act (NI) 2016.In the last 12 months, my Department has provided strategic policy leadership to ensure community planning is delivered consistently in every council area, continued to invest in regeneration projects across the region, and worked with partners to progress more than £1.3 billion of investment through the four City and Growth Deals.Around £21 million has been directed to disadvantaged communities through programmes such as Neighbourhood Renewal, Areas at Risk and Small Pockets of Deprivation. Employability support has been delivered in every council area, including Work Coach services, employer engagement, tailored programmes and Labour Market Partnership funding.My Department has also progressed heritage regeneration and capital works across multiple sites, expanded heritage skills and training, and improved accessibility through inclusive events and multilingual resources. £369,000 has been allocated via the Community Festivals Fund, using a populationbased formula with a deprivation weighting, enabling councils to support communityled cultural festivals across the region.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure (i) for her assessment of her Department's regional balance policy; and (ii) to detail any actions she has delivered to support regional balance, in the last 12 months.
answer_text: Highquality infrastructure is essential to unlocking economic potential, reducing regional imbalance and driving positive change for everyone. That is why promoting regional balance remains one of my department’s core priorities.I am determined to deliver more for the people here and to improve the delivery of regional infrastructure across all six counties. In the last 12 months my department has already:Invested £430,000 in a flood alleviation scheme on the Elmwood Green Stream, Castlederg.Advanced works on the £60m Portadown Flood Alleviation Scheme.Secured an additional £11 million to help unlock wastewater capacity which has enabled over 3,000 new wastewater connections in the Derry area.Secured £10m for the Winter Recovery Road Fund to fund vital repairs across the entire rural and urban road network.Delivered a £430k active travel scheme for Cookstown. Ring-fenced £5.9m in funding to maintain progression on the phase 3 of track improvement between Coleraine and Derry.Undertaken road improvement schemes in: Castlederg and Ederney, Madden, Armoy, Limavady, Randalstown and Rathfriland; andPublished the Rail Project Prioritisation Strategy to progress important rail projects including from Portadown to Derry and Portadown to ArmaghWork also continues on important regional roads schemes including: A4 Enniskillen Bypass, A1 junction improvements, A29 Cookstown Bypass and the A5 Western Transport Corridor.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to detail any measures being introduced to the Planning Department to improve planning performance and service.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health for his assessment of whether existing gynecology services in the Western Health and Social Care Trust have sufficient capacity to meet patient demand.
To ask the Minister of Health to detail any additional resources he plans to allocate to gynaecology services in the Western Health and Social Care Trust to address waiting times.
To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs, pursuant to AQW 30184/22-27, for an update on the reopening of Cladagh Glen, including any associated costs.
answer_text: Initial cost estimates to reinstate the public path ranged from £36,000 to £84,000.  However, safety concerns remain given the level and frequency of erosion being experienced on site, the ongoing risk of further collapse of the path and potential for further landslip in the area. Consequently, appropriate geotechnical expertise has been sought to provide a specialist evaluation of this risk, which can then inform a sustainable approach to the reinstatement of the public path. Whilst a specialist contractor has been identified and engaged, officials are awaiting confirmation of costs for this work prior to commissioning the risk evaluation.I am therefore unable to advise at this time as to the level of all associated costs and when the site will be re-opened for safe public access.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail any engagement he has had with the Department for Culture, Media and Sport, regarding the UK Multi-Sport Grassroots Facilities Programme, in the last 12 months.
To ask the Minister for Communities for an update on the Grassroots Facilities Programme, as part of the Northern Ireland Football Fund.
To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether she is aware of the number of Event Duration Monitors that have been installed by NI Water, during the current PC21 period.
answer_text: The number of Event Duration Monitors (EDMs) installed is an operational matter for NI Water. NI Water has advised that it has installed 759 EDMs to date. 448 of these have been installed so far, within the PC21 period (2021-2027).

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any ongoing work her Department is undertaking for the design of a domestic energy efficiency and heat programme.
answer_text: My Department has consulted on the future support for low carbon heating in residential properties.  The formal government response to the consultation was published on 26th January. Following the consultation, my Department commenced work with external consultants to support the development of a design plan for a domestic energy efficiency and low carbon heat support scheme. This work remains ongoing with the design and related funding options due to be consulted on in the coming months.As policy relating to domestic energy efficiency and heat is cross-cutting, my Department and DfC co-chair a residential decarbonisation coordination group to ensure co-design is embedded in policy development. The group brings together members of wider stakeholders including Utility Regulator, Consumer Council for NI, NI Housing Executive and DoF. A “Schemes Subgroup” is also in place with membership from these stakeholder groups, to help ensure further collaboration and co-ordination in the development of future retrofit schemes.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail any work currently being undertaken by her Department on reviewing permitted development rights for telecommunications.
answer_text: My officials have commenced early policy development into reviewing the permitted development rights (PDRs) for telecommunications. This has and will involve extensive stakeholder engagement at the appropriate times, including with the mobile industry, which is ongoing, as these proposals develop.  												Any recommendations arising from the outworkings of the Barrier Busting Taskforce (BBT) and its Planning Subgroup will be brought to me and other key stakeholders in due course.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail when the responses to the consultation on support for low carbon heat in the residential sector will be published.
answer_text: My Department published the Government Response to Support for Low Carbon Heat in the Residential Sector Consultation on 26th January 2026 and can be found on the Departments website.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether she will ensure that the shortfall from her Department regarding the A4 Enniskillen Southern Bypass project, arising from the surrender of £6.6 million for the scheme, which forms part of the Mid South West Growth Deal, will be rectified before the signing of the Mid South West financial agreement, which is expected by the end of 2026.
answer_text: The Minister of Finance has confirmed that projects for which there is an Executive Commitment, such as the A4 Enniskillen Southern Bypass will receive the ringfenced funding in future years.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy (i) for her assessment of his Department's regional balance policy; and (ii) to detail any actions he has delivered to support regional balance, in the last 12 months.
answer_text: The Sub Regional Economic Plan, published in October 2024 set out a new strategic approach to addressing Regional Balance.A key element of the Plan has been the establishment of Local Economic Partnerships in each council area, to develop action plans aimed at providing locally identified solutions to locally identified economic needs. The LEPs have access to a dedicated Regional Balance Fund of £45 million over 3 years. During the course of the last 12 months all 11 Local Economic Partnerships have been established and a number of action plans approved; with Councils starting to deliver projects locally to improve Regional Balance. Invest NI has adopted a new regional model and recruited 5 new Heads of Regional Business located across its regional offices over the last 12 months.  Progress continues on building out Invest NI’s regional teams, allowing the agency to increase the number of businesses it can work with regionally.I am encouraged to see, between 2024 and 2025, the regional median earnings gap between the highest and lowest earning council area fell by two percentage points, highlighting that progress is being made.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs (i) for his assessment of his Department's regional balance policy; and (ii) to detail any actions he has delivered to support regional balance, in the last 12 months.
answer_text: While my Department does not operate a standalone regional balance policy, my officials and I continue to implement numerous strategies and programmes that align with DAERA’s statutory responsibilities, encompassing areas such as food, farming, environment, fisheries, forestry, and rural affairs. These interventions inherently support and make a positive contribution toward the Executive's shared objective of achieving regional balance across Northern Ireland.   As you will be aware, I have committed, with the support of my Executive colleagues, to developing for consultation in March 2026, a new evidence-based rural policy that recognises that all Executive departments are responsible for exercising their functions in both rural and urban areas.  This work ensures that my Department continues to make a significant contribution to improving outcomes in line with the Executive’s ambition to achieve regional balance throughout Northern Ireland.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail any (i) engagements; and (ii) meetings, she has (a) accepted; and (b) refused, since she took up her post in February 2025.
answer_text: Since taking up office and until 11 February 2026, I have had a total of 316 engagements and meetings.  Details of my meetings with external organisations are published on a quarterly basis on the Open Data NI Portal (https://www.opendatani.gov.uk/search-global?q=meetings). This currently holds information up to December 2025.Over the same period, I have declined a total of 241 invitations.  This figure includes all invitations to both internal and external meetings, events, visits conferences etc received. Information held centrally in my Private Office does not distinguish between meetings and engagements within these categories.Invitations are declined for a range of routine reasons, including diary pressures, competing departmental commitments, or where requests fall outside the scope of my ministerial responsibilities.It would not be appropriate to comment on individual cases. I can assure the Member that all requests are considered carefully and consistently, in line with established procedures and the effective management of ministerial time.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the reason why the Enniskillen A4 Southern Bypass Project has been paused due to climate change legislation passed in 2022, given that the lands for this project were vested by her Department in July 2021, prior to the Climate Change Act (Northern Ireland) 2022 being enacted.
answer_text: The Enniskillen Bypass project is no different to other major infrastructure projects being progressed by my Department, all of which are being considered in light of the A5 Judgement.While Statutory Orders were made in 2021 and initial works have been completed, it is appropriate to allow time for completion of legal proceedings so that the Department might fully understand the Court of Appeal’s ruling.  I therefore consider it prudent to reflect those lessons across other major road schemes, including the A4 Enniskillen Southern Bypass, to avoid further challenge and exposure to legal and financial risk.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance (i) for his assessment of his Department's regional balance policy; and (ii) to detail any actions he has delivered to support regional balance, in the last 12 months.
answer_text: I am fully committed to driving regional balance for those areas within my responsibility as Finance Minister, to ensure all areas and people are able to share in the benefits of economic prosperity.For example, my department continues to work with partners to progress the four City and Growth Deals, collectively representing a strategic investment of over £1 billion across all 11 council areas. These Deals are a key mechanism for delivering the Executive’s commitment to regional balance, ensuring that economic opportunity and prosperity are more evenly distributed across the North.My department has also implemented a Hybrid Working policy, which helps support regional balance by affording our workforce with more choice about their working location, allowing a mix of workplace, home, and remote working. Additionally, seven regional Connect 2 hubs are operational across the North and may be used by civil servants as an alternative to their designated workplace on an exceptional basis.In addition, the recent Pilot AO recruitment initiative in the North West has resulted in 155 successful candidates being appointed to permanent positions, with their employment commencing in January 2026. A merit list has also been established to facilitate the filling of both current and future vacancies, and applicants indicated preferred working patterns and locations to allow flexibility throughout the life cycle of the list.Further, our independent statistical agency, NISRA, continues to deliver data and analysis to provide robust regional evidence to inform policy making and monitoring right across a suite of Executive policies and actions.I will continue to collaborate with my Executive colleagues to support all citizens and help ensure we reach our full potential.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the amount of the recently announced £7.85 million Winter Recovery Road Fund that will be allocated to the Fermanagh section.
answer_text: The £7.85m Winter Recovery Road Fund includes a £3.6m allocation for Western division which comprises Fermanagh section office. Budgets are recorded on a divisional basis rather than by section office. This additional funding will deliver an enhanced structural maintenance programme including large-scale patching and resurfacing targeted at those areas of greatest need. This work will commence without delay.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on her plans to introduce legislation to prohibit the overtaking of school buses at bus stops.
answer_text: Work is progressing on the consultation for the legislation, and I will make an announcement in the coming weeks. This is a priority for me. and it will be introduced in this mandate.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether she plans to introduce legislation which will prohibit overtaking school buses in the 2022-27 Assembly mandate.
answer_text: Work is progressing on the consultation for the legislation, and I will make an announcement in the coming weeks. This is a priority for me. and it will be introduced in this mandate.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether she will publish a timetable for the introduction of legislation which will prohibit overtaking school buses.
answer_text: Work is progressing on the consultation for the legislation, and I will make an announcement in the coming weeks. This is a priority for me. and it will be introduced in this mandate.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the rationale as to why the A4 Enniskillen Southern Bypass Project was the sole infrastructure project where money has been surrendered, as part of the Further Allocations 2025-26, announced by the Minister of Finance on 6 February 2026.
answer_text: I would refer the Member to my answers to AQW 38674 and 38675/22-27 in relation to AQW 39959 and 39961/22-27.The Enniskillen Bypass project is no different to other major infrastructure projects being progressed by my Department, all of which are being considered in light of the A5 Judgement.I understand the benefits that infrastructure schemes bring to communities and the wider travelling public,  however I must also be mindful of the best use of the limited resources we have available in the department and make appropriate judgements around their use.We have to await the Court of Appeal's ruling, because it is important that we consider it fully when looking at the next steps for all major roads’ projects, as we do not want to be in a scenario where we face challenges on everything. Officials continue to work to keep this important scheme live.As is normal process, a number of other major projects within my portfolio have also had funding returned as part of monitoring rounds in the 2025/26 year.The Minister of Finance has confirmed that projects for which there is an Executive Commitment, such as the A4 Enniskillen Southern Bypass will receive the ringfenced funding in future years.Whilst I appreciate that this decision will be disappointing for all those who are keen to see the bypass built, I remain committed to the delivery of the scheme.

answer_text: I would refer the Member to my answers to AQW 38674 and 38675/22-27 in relation to AQW 39959 and 39961/22-27.The Enniskillen Bypass project is no different to other major infrastructure projects being progressed by my Department, all of which are being considered in light of the A5 Judgement.I understand the benefits that infrastructure schemes bring to communities and the wider travelling public,  however I must also be mindful of the best use of the limited resources we have available in the department and make appropriate judgements around their use.We have to await the Court of Appeal's ruling, because it is important that we consider it fully when looking at the next steps for all major roads’ projects, as we do not want to be in a scenario where we face challenges on everything. Officials continue to work to keep this important scheme live.As is normal process, a number of other major projects within my portfolio have also had funding returned as part of monitoring rounds in the 2025/26 year.The Minister of Finance has confirmed that projects for which there is an Executive Commitment, such as the A4 Enniskillen Southern Bypass will receive the ringfenced funding in future years.Whilst I appreciate that this decision will be disappointing for all those who are keen to see the bypass built, I remain committed to the delivery of the scheme.
